# Contact CTA and Footer — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## User Story

**As a** prospective client who has reviewed the landing page content,  
**I want to** see a clear, visually distinct call-to-action panel and a clean footer with navigation links,  
**so that** I can take the next step of getting in touch without having to search for contact details.

---

## Description

This story delivers two closely related sections at the bottom of the landing page:

1. **Contact CTA Panel** — A full-width accent gradient section with white text and a prominent email-based CTA. Contact details (email address) are sourced from `site-properties.json` to ensure consistency across the site.
2. **Footer** — A lightweight bar with anchor links to page sections and social placeholder links.

**Business value:** Ending the page with a visually prominent CTA maximises the chance of converting an engaged visitor into an inquiry. A footer with anchor links improves navigability.

---

## Scenario

### Scenario 1: Visitor scrolls to the Contact CTA section

**Given** a visitor reaches the bottom area of the page  
**When** the Contact CTA section enters the viewport  
**Then** a full-width panel is displayed with the accent gradient background  
**And** a heading and supporting copy are visible in white text  
**And** a CTA button or email link is displayed, showing the contact email from `site-properties.json`

### Scenario 2: Visitor clicks the email CTA

**Given** the visitor sees the Contact CTA panel  
**When** they click the email CTA  
**Then** the visitor's default email client opens with the contact email pre-populated in the To field  
**And** no backend form submission or server call is made

### Scenario 3: Contact email is updated in site-properties.json

**Given** the contact email is changed in `site-properties.json`  
**When** the page is rebuilt/reloaded  
**Then** the updated email appears in the CTA panel without any code changes to the component

### Scenario 4: Visitor interacts with the footer

**Given** the visitor is at the bottom of the page  
**When** they see the footer  
**Then** anchor links to key page sections (e.g., Services, How We Work, Technologies, Contact) are visible  
**And** social placeholder links are displayed (may link to `#` until real URLs are configured)  
**And** the footer does not visually compete with the Contact CTA panel above it

### Scenario 5: Visitor uses a footer anchor link

**Given** the footer is visible  
**When** the visitor clicks an anchor link in the footer  
**Then** the page scrolls smoothly to the corresponding section  
**And** no full-page reload occurs

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Contact CTA Panel — Core Functionality
- [ ] The Contact CTA section is rendered at or near the bottom of the page, above the footer
- [ ] Section background uses the accent gradient defined in design tokens (matches hero marker and process numbers)
- [ ] Heading and body copy are rendered in white text
- [ ] A CTA is displayed: an `mailto:` link or button that opens the visitor's email client
- [ ] The contact email address is sourced from `site-properties.json`; no hardcoded email in the component

### Contact CTA Panel — Accessibility
- [ ] White text on the accent gradient background meets WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 minimum for body text)
- [ ] The email CTA has a visible focus indicator
- [ ] The CTA label is descriptive (e.g., "Email us at [address]" rather than just "Click here")

### Footer — Core Functionality
- [ ] Footer contains anchor links to at least: Services (`#services`), How We Work, Technologies, and Contact (`#contact`)
- [ ] Social placeholder links are present (can target `#` as placeholder)
- [ ] Footer renders below the Contact CTA panel

### Footer — User Experience
- [ ] Anchor links scroll smoothly to their target sections
- [ ] Footer is visually lightweight and does not distract from the CTA panel above it
- [ ] Footer links have visible focus states for keyboard navigation

### Content Consistency
- [ ] Contact email is read from `site-properties.json` in both the Contact CTA and footer (if shown in footer)
- [ ] Updating `site-properties.json` is sufficient to propagate the email change site-wide

### Responsive Layout
- [ ] On desktop: CTA panel and footer use full-width layouts
- [ ] On mobile (<768px): CTA heading and button stack vertically; footer links wrap without overflow
- [ ] No horizontal scrollbar at any viewport width

---

## Prerequisites

- [Hero Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./hero-section-landing-page.md) — accent gradient token must be defined before the CTA panel can use it
- [Services Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./services-section-landing-page.md) — `#services` anchor must exist for footer navigation to work
- [How We Work Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./how-we-work-section-landing-page.md) — section anchor must exist for footer navigation
- [Technologies Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./technologies-section-landing-page.md) — section anchor must exist for footer navigation
